title: Colossians: New Self Living
objective: Practice putting off the old self and putting on Christlike virtues in home and public life.

required_passages.text: Set your minds on things that are above... put to death what is earthly... put on compassionate hearts... whatever you do... do everything in the name of the Lord Jesus.

segments.audio_script: Union with Christ produces practical transformation in speech, relationships, and work.

Listen to Colossians 3-4. Paul tells believers to seek things above because they have died and risen with Christ. They must put to death sin and put on compassion, humility, patience, forgiveness, and love. He applies this to households, work, and prayerful witness toward outsiders.
Colossian believers needed a clear path from doctrine to daily discipleship.
Sanctification is identity-driven: become what you are in Christ. Mission credibility grows where speech and relationships reflect gospel character.
Key themes include new self, holiness, forgiveness, Christ-centered households, vocational faithfulness, prayer, and witness.

Review question: What does Paul tell the Colossians to put on in Colossians 3? Answer: compassion, kindness, humility, meekness, and patience, bearing with one another and forgiving each other. Over all these put on love.
segments.correct_answer: compassion, kindness, humility, meekness, patience, bearing with one another, forgiving, and love above all
acceptable_variants: put on the new self: compassion kindness humility meekness patience — and over all love
colossians 3 — the new self is clothed in christ-like virtues with love as the outermost garment
bearing with and forgiving one another and binding all together in love

segments.audio_script: The risen life reshapes everything. Next, Thessalonians teaches hope and endurance under pressure.

vocabulary.definition: The instructions in Colossians 3:18-4:1 for wives, husbands, children, fathers, slaves, and masters; Paul structures relationships within the existing social order while reframing them around Christ
The teaching in Colossians 2:9-10 that all the fullness of deity dwells bodily in Christ and believers are complete in him; it refutes any claim that supplementary wisdom or practices are needed
